title: Implement a reproduction assist mode that captures commands and input files into an output script
body:
Yosys can be embedded deeply into a build system as it's likely one of the last stages that get run. This makes it likely to be embedded deeply into a build such that yosys might be provided dynamically generated HDL from the build system or have a script that's dynamically generated from a large set of source files. Neither of these are nice to deal with when attempting to submit an issue with yosys for debugging. 

In order to simply capturing reproduction, yosys could have a mode that writes out a reproducing script, which contains both the commands and directly embeds the files used by those commands.

The challenge here is of course that yosys has an extremely flexible frontend that makes it intractable to fully implement this kind of feature (stuff like ffi and arbitrary tcl isn't necessarily reasonable to capture), however most workflows that I'm aware of do not execute yosys in this way, so just the most obvious captures should provide the majority of the desired functionality.

Original issue report 

> Since in the process of debugging issues within yosys it might only be reproducible as part of a potentially multilayer flow of upstream elements it seems prudent to implement some kind of argument or setting (probably a command line argument) to allow turning yosys into a dummy mode where it will simply record to a file enough state to ingest the same form and reproduce the response to the original state. 
> 
> Ideally this mode could be coupled with actually working as intended in order to not trip up processes that would fail if artifacts are not produced.
> 
> Hopefully this is both not a totally stupid idea and also isn't already a feature that I've missed somehow, but it would be extremely useful. I'm sure other solutions could exist but I can't imagine them working nearly as well as giving yosys this functionality internally, internal support would allow capturing the complete set of both input files and transformation at runtime such that even ephemeral input data was capturable.
> 
> For an example of what I can imagine would require this, imagine a system where code is developed locally and yosys runs on a build server. Or some kind of parallelized build (or at least validation) where independent ip blocks are generated/verified in parallel. These kinds of configs would likely be totally dynamic and extremely difficult to reproduce the exact sequence of steps leading up to the issue.
>
